title: Acrostichum fasciculatum
intro: Acrostichum fasciculatum, also known as the chain fern, is a species of fern that is commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. This plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Acrostichum fasciculatum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Acrostichum fasciculatum is commonly used to make preparations such as decoctions, infusions, and ointments, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including fever, cough, and skin infections.
taxonomy: Acrostichum fasciculatum is a species of fern in the family Lomariopsidaceae. This plant is a type of vascular plant that belongs to the division Pteridophyta. As a member of the Pteridophyta division, Acrostichum fasciculatum shares characteristics with other ferns. The classification of Acrostichum fasciculatum places it within the kingdom Plantae. It is also classified as a member of the phylum Tracheophyta, which includes all vascular plants.
